body.purpose: Table logs, inputs, descriptions, and summaries.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.value: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Used for main calls to action, form submissions, and primary dashboard operations.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background-color: #4f46e5; color: #ffffff; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #4338ca; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { .ds-btn-primary { background-color: #818cf8; color: #020617; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #a5b4fc; } }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard input field for search queries and form entry.
components.css: .ds-input-text { background-color: transparent; color: #0f172a; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 10px; outline: none; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; transition: border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-input-text:focus { border-color: #4f46e5; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { .ds-input-text { color: #f8fafc; border-color: #1e293b; } .ds-input-text:focus { border-color: #818cf8; } }

components.name: Violation Status Badge
components.description: Tag used to call out active violations or unexcused absence (Alpha) status.
components.css: .ds-tag-violation { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background-color: rgba(225, 29, 72, 0.1); color: #e11d48; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 6px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { .ds-tag-violation { background-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.1); color: #f87171; } }

narrative.overview: The Kawal design system represents stability, structure, and institutional reliability. Designed for guidance counselors, homeroom teachers, and school administrators, the interface acts as a definitive source of truth. The interface is optimized for high-density, high-legibility layout patterns that allow staff to log records quickly during a fast-paced school day. Kawal explicitly rejects cartoonish illustrations, overly spacious card-based layouts, and playful color gradients.
keyCharacteristics: High-density information grids and clear border demarcations.
Context-specific color-coding for behavioral severity and attendance status.
Minimalist flat layout that maximizes content scanning speed.

rules.name: The Alert Rarity Rule
rules.body: Status colors (rose, orange, emerald) must only be used on badges, tags, or indicator lights to draw attention. Never use status colors as background fills for large areas.

rules.name: The Context-Preservation Rule
rules.body: Emerald is strictly reserved for 'Present/Hadir' and 'Approved' states. Rose is strictly for 'Alpha' and 'Violations'. Never swap them or use them for unrelated actions.

rules.name: The Readable Data Rule
rules.body: Body text for tables and student logs must maintain a line-height of at least 1.5 to guarantee rapid scanning without ocular fatigue.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Box shadows are forbidden on standard containers. A subtle border-color shift or background tint must be used instead to show container structure.

dos: Do use 1px border lines and background tone shifts to separate data sections.
Do keep container corners rounded at exactly 10px (rounded-xl or {rounded.md}).
Do ensure text contrast matches WCAG AA (at least 4.5:1 ratio) on both light and dark themes.
Do use text-wrap: balance on display and section headings to prevent awkward orphans.
donts: Don't use cartoonish or overly playful UI patterns that compromise professional authority.
Don't use cluttered or fragmented layouts that resemble legacy school software.
Don't use generic AI/SaaS design tropes like purple-to-blue gradients, glassmorphism blurs, or saturated warm cream backgrounds.
Don't use drop shadows with blur radius greater than 4px on cards or containers.
Don't use side-stripe borders (e.g., border-left thicker than 1px) on alerts or cards.
